summary: In trauma patients after high-energy collisions involving significant neck pain without neurological symptoms, prompt cervical spine immobilization remains essential due to risk of undiagnosed spinal injuries despite normal initial neuromuscular findings. This approach aligns with standard protocols emphasizing early protection over assumption of safety based solely on symptomatology.
